name: Meatballs with Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Cheddar cheese (cut into 1/4 inch cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind the sirloin and dice the cheese into 1/4 inch cubes.
2. Mix onion powder, garlic powder, and cayenne pepper with the eggs, ground meat, and sirloin until combined.
3. Roll the mixture into 2-1/2 inch balls, placing a cube of cheese in the center of each ball before reclosing it.
4. Dust the meatballs with flour to coat lightly.
5. Heat cooking oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
6. Add chopped onion and cook until softened.
7. Add green peppers and continue cooking for a few minutes.
8. Stir 2 teaspoons of flour into the oil to make a roux, then add tomato sauce and water. Simmer for 10 minutes.
9. Add the meatballs and simmer until the gravy reaches the desired thickness.
10. Pour in white wine and stir to combine.
11. Serve over spaghetti or rice with a salad. Serves 8.
Note: This recipe was sourced from the 1979 New Orleans Times-Picayune Recipe Contest Cookbook and typed for convenience by Nancy Coleman.
